title: pilosella paragoga subsp. biformis
intro: Pilosella paragoga subsp. biformis is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, Pilosella paragoga subsp. biformis has been prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
